preflight.note: Created before implementation work starts for a provider-neutral email channel adapter derived from CLV-018 roadmap records.

acceptance_criteria.text: Define provider-neutral email channel contracts for outbound mail, inbound mail normalization, attachments, delivery/failure state, credential validation, and disabled states without coupling ForgeLink to one mail provider.

Implement secure credential storage/configuration for SMTP plus an explicit inbound option such as IMAP polling or provider webhook, with credentials redacted from renderer, logs, diagnostics, exports, and screenshots.

Implement outbound email sending behind the channel registry with deterministic tests for success, provider rejection, missing credentials, invalid recipient, attachment bounds, retryable failure, and permanent failure.

Implement inbound email normalization when enabled, including duplicate detection, contact resolution through email contact points, bounded body/attachment handling, and clear disabled state when inbound is not configured.
Add UI for email channel configuration and contact email identities without making email the default human loop or granting approval privileges automatically.
Define and test quick-action boundaries for email replies or signed links so approval/dismiss actions require anti-replay, expiration, contact policy, and local pending-action verification.

Ensure data safety: email messages and attachments participate in backup/export/retention as private communication data, while support diagnostics exclude bodies, addresses, headers, attachments, and provider IDs by default.
Document operator setup, privacy limits, likely provider requirements, failure modes, testing strategy, and the exact features shipped versus deferred.
